summary: Implemented and typed several gm/mn utility functions in game-mode, Rules menu, and Name Entry code. The diff replaces stubs for fn_8018846C, mn_80232458, mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_802388D4, updates headers, and adds supporting Rules-menu data arrays. decomp-dev reported 4 new full matches totaling +620 matched bytes; mnName_GetColumnCount was listed as a 98.72% improvement despite the PR body claiming all functions were verified 100% via objdiff.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c — implemented fn_8018846C result/ranking-flow callback logic using lbl_80473700, pl_8004134C, and Player_GetPlayerCharacter
src/melee/gm/gm_1832.h — replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototype with int fn_8018846C(void)
src/melee/mn/mnname.c — implemented Name Entry page count, column count, and HSD_JObj lookup/layout helper
src/melee/mn/mnname.h — added concrete prototypes for mnName_GetPageCount, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_802388D4
src/melee/mn/mnruleplus.c — added zero-initialized AnimLoopSettings/data arrays and implemented mn_80232458 Rules-menu animation-loop selector
what_changed: fn_8018846C now returns int, calls pl_8004134C(0), updates a per-character slot in lbl_80473700 using Player_GetPlayerCharacter(0) + 2, caches nonzero results in lbl_80473700[67]/[68], and returns the cached result when present.
mnName_GetPageCount now counts valid name slots over 0x78 entries using IsNameValid((u8)i) and returns ceil(valid_count / 24), matching the PR body’s Name Entry pagination description.
mnName_GetColumnCount now counts the same 0x78 valid name slots and returns ceil(valid_count / 6); the implementation includes PAD_STACK(16), indicating a stack-shaping match tactic.

mnName_802388D4 now maps Name Entry layout indices to HSD_JObj pointers: indices below 0x18 walk the child/next chain from the JObj pointer at gobj + 0x30, while indices 0x18, 0x19, and 0x1A return JObj pointers from gobj offsets 0x24, 0x18, and 0x1C.

mn_80232458 now returns an AnimLoopSettings pointer for Rules-menu options 1 through 4, rejects other options with NULL, reads mn_803ED2E8[option][1], and selects from mn_803ED270 or mn_803ED294 based on direction and value.
mnruleplus.c gained supporting zero-initialized globals mn_803ED270[3], mn_803ED294[7], and mn_803ED2E8[16][2], improving the unit’s .data match according to the bot report.
Headers were synchronized with concrete return and argument types, replacing generic UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations.
smart_moves: Kept implementation and header prototypes in sync, converting unknown declarations to concrete signatures as soon as the function behavior was understood.
Used simple loop-and-remainder ceiling division for Name Entry pagination instead of algebraic shortcuts, preserving the likely original compiler structure.
Preserved unsigned-byte behavior with explicit u8 casts on Name Entry indices, which matters for comparisons, loop limits, and switch cases.
Handled JObj traversal defensively with NULL-propagating child/next access, matching both HSD object semantics and likely branch structure.
Added the exact supporting Rules-menu data arrays needed by mn_80232458 rather than treating the function body in isolation; this also moved .data closer to matching.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in mnName_GetColumnCount as a targeted decomp stack-layout tactic.
decomp_lessons: For menu pagination code, the Name Entry roster evidence in this PR uses 0x78 total slots, pages of 24, and groups of 6; future mnName work should reuse those constants cautiously where the same list is being counted.

When a function returns a pointer into HSD_GObj-owned data before the struct is fully named, raw u8* offset access can be an acceptable interim matching tactic, but follow-up struct field recovery is still desirable.

Data matching can be required for a function match: mn_80232458 depended on newly declared AnimLoopSettings arrays and a u8 lookup table, and the bot separately reported a mnruleplus .data improvement.
Concrete prototypes are useful decomp evidence. This PR narrowed fn_8018846C to int(void), mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount to s32(void), and mnName_802388D4 to HSD_JObj*(HSD_GObj*, u8).

Do not rely on PR titles or bodies alone for exact match counts. Here the title and bot report say 4 new full matches, while the body lists five functions and claims all were 100%; mnName_GetColumnCount appears only as a 98.72% improvement in the bot output.
naming_conventions: Module prefixes were preserved: gm_ / fn_ for game-mode symbols, mnName_ for Name Entry helpers, and mn_ for broader menu/rules code.
Descriptive names were used where behavior was clear: mnName_GetPageCount and mnName_GetColumnCount describe the counted pagination results.
Address-suffixed placeholder names remained where semantics were not fully established, such as fn_8018846C, mnName_802388D4, mn_80232458, mn_803ED270, mn_803ED294, and mn_803ED2E8.
Known no-argument functions were declared with explicit void rather than UNK_PARAMS.
Unknown global storage such as lbl_80473700 was left under its existing label name rather than over-naming weakly understood state.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PAD_STACK(16) was inserted in mnName_GetColumnCount to shape stack usage.
Ceiling division was expressed as a modulo test assigning an extra variable, then count / divisor + extra, matching a common original-code pattern.
Explicit casts to u8 were retained for loop arguments and index comparisons in mnName code.
JObj chain traversal used ternary NULL checks for child and next links, likely preserving branch/codegen shape.
mn_80232458 used direct array indexing and pointer returns into fixed-size AnimLoopSettings arrays, with option validation written as chained != comparisons.
fn_8018846C used local pointer aliases into lbl_80473700, including ptr[67] and ptr[68], rather than abstracting the global state into a new struct.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code at 58.00% (+0.02%, +620 bytes). New full matches: mnName_GetPageCount (+196), mnName_802388D4 (+144), fn_8018846C (+140), and mn_80232458 (+140).
decomp-dev[bot] also reported mnName_GetColumnCount as an unmatched-item improvement to 98.72% (+185) and mnruleplus .data as improved from 59.85% to 67.65% (+28).

The PR body states all functions were verified 100% via objdiff and ninja built cleanly; this conflicts with the bot’s mnName_GetColumnCount percentage, so the exact final status of that symbol is uncertain from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Verify final merged match status for mnName_GetColumnCount, because the PR body says 100% but the decomp-dev report lists 98.72%.
Recover or confirm MnName_GObj field names for raw offsets 0x18, 0x1C, 0x24, and 0x30 used by mnName_802388D4.
Investigate semantic names for mn_803ED270, mn_803ED294, and mn_803ED2E8 in Rules-menu animation-loop logic.
Trace callers of fn_8018846C to identify the meaning of lbl_80473700 slots 67 and 68 in the results/ranking flow.
Check whether the title’s '4 functions' intentionally excludes mnName_GetColumnCount as a near-match rather than a full match.
